output.answer: The US Constitution amendments that do not concern slaves or voting rights are the First through Tenth Amendments (the Bill of Rights): 1st Amendment (freedom of religion, speech, press, assembly, and petition), 2nd Amendment (right to bear arms), 3rd Amendment (quartering of soldiers), 4th Amendment (searches and seizures), 5th Amendment (due process), 6th Amendment (right to a fair trial), 7th Amendment (civil jury trials), 8th Amendment (bail and punishments), 9th Amendment (unenumerated rights), and 10th Amendment (powers reserved to states).

args.answer: The US Constitution amendments that do not concern slaves or voting rights are the First through Tenth Amendments (the Bill of Rights): 1st Amendment (freedom of religion, speech, press, assembly, and petition), 2nd Amendment (right to bear arms), 3rd Amendment (quartering of soldiers), 4th Amendment (searches and seizures), 5th Amendment (due process), 6th Amendment (right to a fair trial), 7th Amendment (civil jury trials), 8th Amendment (bail and punishments), 9th Amendment (unenumerated rights), and 10th Amendment (powers reserved to states).
